text.title: Contractor Audition Rubric Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Contractor Audition Rubric application must enforce. Hard rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Apply Contractor Audition Rubric only to a single instance of the upstream playbook task. Never batch multiple unrelated decisions through one pass.
rule.rationale: Batching turns a 1-decision artefact into a debate club. Each instance gets its own record so the rationale is traceable per case.

rule.statement: Every input field has a declared type and a named source (path / URL / artefact id). Free-form prose without a source citation is rejected.
rule.rationale: Untyped inputs let LLMs hallucinate plausible-but-wrong values. Typed + sourced inputs make the output falsifiable.

rule.statement: The output names a single accountable owner (handle, email, or org role). No `team`, `we`, `us`, or `engineering` as the owner value.
rule.rationale: Plural ownership = no ownership. Future readers need a named human to chase when the artefact is stale.

rule.statement: Output carries semver version and `last_reviewed` ISO date. Records older than 90 days without review must be flagged on read.
rule.rationale: Methodology outputs decay; treating them as immutable is a defect. Versioning + review-by-date forces refresh cadence.

rule.statement: The `rationale` field cites at least one input artefact by name; the `inputs_used` list is non-empty unless decision == `no-op`.
rule.rationale: Decisions made without a citation chain cannot be audited or reversed safely. Citations bind the output to its evidence.
